revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23392883474|time triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Doi%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{lowercase title}} {{documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} This template produces a simple, standardized link to a [[:en:Digital object identifier|Digital object identifier]] (DOI). == Usage == :{{doi|10.1016/j.coi.2004.08.001}} which produces: :{{doi|10.1016/j.coi.2004.08.001}} If the doi is freely-accessible, you can tag it as free with {{para|doi-access|free}} :{{doi|10.1038/495426a|doi-access=free}} which produces: :{{doi|10.1038/495426a|doi-access=free}} For instructions on how to use Wikipedia to automatically expand a DOI into a full reference complete with article title, author(s), date, journal name, volume, issue, page numbers, etc., please see [[User:Citation bot/use]]. The parameter {{para|nolink}}, if set to any value (e.g. "true"), will suppress the "doi:" prefix in front of the identifier. To mark a DOI accessible to the general public, use {{para|doi-access|free}}. ==See also== *{{tl|doi-inline}} *{{tl|hdl}} *[[Wikipedia:Digital Object Identifier]] *[[Wikipedia:ISBN]] *[[Wikipedia:PMID]] *[[Wikipedia:ISSN]] *{{tl|bibcode}} *{{tl|PII}} *{{tl|SSRN}} {{sandbox other|1=|2= }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "DOI name", "example": "10.1000/xyz123", "required": true }, "id": {} }, "description": "This template produces a simple, standardized link to a digital object identifier (DOI).", "paramOrder": [ "1", "id" ] } [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{lowercase title}} {{documentation subpage}} {{high-use}} This template produces a simple, standardized link to a [[:en:Digital object identifier|Digital object identifier]] (DOI). == Usage == :{{doi|10.1016/j.coi.2004.08.001}} which produces: :{{doi|10.1016/j.coi.2004.08.001}} If the doi is freely-accessible, you can tag it as free with {{para|doi-access|free}} :{{doi|10.1038/495426a|doi-access=free}} which produces: :{{doi|10.1038/495426a|doi-access=free}} For instructions on how to use Wikipedia to automatically expand a DOI into a full reference complete with article title, author(s), date, journal name, volume, issue, page numbers, etc., please see [[User:Citation bot/use]]. The parameter {{para|nolink}}, if set to any value (e.g. "true"), will suppress the "doi:" prefix in front of the identifier. To mark a DOI accessible to the general public, use {{para|doi-access|free}}. ==See also== *{{tl|doi-inline}} *{{tl|hdl}} *[[Wikipedia:Digital Object Identifier]] *[[Wikipedia:ISBN]] *[[Wikipedia:PMID]] *[[Wikipedia:ISSN]] *{{tl|bibcode}} *{{tl|PII}} *{{tl|SSRN}} {{sandbox other|1=|2= }} { "params": { "1": { "label": "DOI name", "example": "10.1000/xyz123", "required": true }, "id": {} }, "description": "This template produces a simple, standardized link to a digital object identifier (DOI).", "paramOrder": [ "1", "id" ] } [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
